description_long: A terracotta-colored, square-tapered flower pot holds a cluster of white-petaled daisies with yellow centers. The pot has a marbled texture and appears to be made of ceramic or clay, filled with dark soil. The flowers are supported by slender, greenish stems, some of which are slightly bent or leaning outward. The daisy blossoms are arranged at varying heights, creating a natural, slightly wild appearance. No leaves are visible among the stems, and the overall look is that of a decorative potted plant suitable for urban settings such as sidewalks, patios, or building entrances. The flowers' bright white petals contrast sharply with the earthy tones of the pot and soil.
description: A marbled terracotta flower pot filled with white daisies on slender stems, arranged in a natural, slightly wild cluster.
description_view_0: Front view shows the marbled pot and a dense cluster of daisies, with several stems leaning outward and the flowers facing forward.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a prominent daisy near the pot's edge and reveals more of the soil surface, with stems appearing more spread out.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the pot's right side and shows the flowers from a slightly different angle, with some stems and blossoms partially obscured.
description_view_3: From this side, the stems appear more separated, and the arrangement of the flowers looks less dense, with more visible gaps between stems.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially around a central yellow disc, forming a symmetrical daisy-like pattern. Each flower head displays a single whorl of petals with slight overlapping at the base, typical of Asteraceae family.
